tmux-agent — iOS native app

The web hub mapped to a native iPhone app. Bottom tab bar as the spine of the IA; each tab is a focused, single-purpose screen — not a shrunken desktop layout. Hierarchy is the same (Project ▸ Feature ▸ Ticket ▸ Session) but expressed as a navigation stack with push transitions, large titles, and grouped lists. The Sessions tab opens to a Runestone-style terminal pane with a chip switcher, quick-keys row, and an input bar.

Tabs
Inbox · Projects · Roadmap · Sessions · You
Type
SF Pro (UI), JetBrains Mono (IDs, code, timestamps)
Accent
iris (default) — toggle via Tweaks
Conventions
iOS 17/26 large titles · grouped inset lists · segmented controls · liquid-glass tab bar
Out of scope
iPad layouts, Apple Watch glances, Live Activities, push notifications
Primary tabs
The five top-level destinations — bottom-tab navigation

Feature docs · PRD editor
9:41
FEAT-018
PRD
Eng design
Decisions
Notes
FEAT-018 · PRD
Agent pane multiplexer
Problem
Running multiple agent sessions today means juggling separate tmux windows. Switching context loses scrollback and breaks the mental model of which agent is on which ticket.
Goal
One window, N panes, each bound to a feature's context bundle, with persistent scrollback search and per-pane state badges.
Acceptance criteria
✓
Up to 6 panes per window with named splits
✓
Scrollback persists across detach/reattach
Per-pane state badge: idle, active, awaiting
Scrollback regex search with hit-jump
Open questions
Should pane layout be saved per-feature or per-workspace? Leaning per-feature so resuming a ticket restores the exact split.
3

Defaulting to split. Reply 'unified', 'split', or 'auto'. ▎

TMX-0050In review
Diff viewer component
session-07 · feat/tmx-0050-diff-viewer · +412 / −37 · 9 files
Diff
Checklist 6/6
Files
diff/renderer.go · split-view sync
@@ -88,6 +88,12 @@ func (r *Renderer) Render() {
     for i, line := range r.lines {
-      r.write(line)
+      l, r := r.split(line)
+      r.writePair(l, r)
+      r.syncScroll(i)
     }
Acceptance · 6 / 6
✓
Side-by-side renders without horizontal scroll lag
✓
Word-level highlight inside changed lines
✓
Scroll syncs across both panes
✓
Collapsible context with `…` markers
✓
Keyboard nav: j/k between hunks
✓
No regression in unified mode
